title: Lesson 349
transcript: Section 5.6, this is one of the new sections added to the CISSP exam in the 2021 update. This is a bit of a long section, so buckle in. Let me ask you a basic question here. If an organization is implementing any sort of authentication system, a single sign-on system, a federated identity management system, a RADIUS or TACAC systems, we'll talk about those in domain four. If an organization is implementing any sort of security, any sort of authentication system, should security be involved? That's the fundamental question. Should security be involved if the organization is implementing any sort of authentication system? What do you think? Yeah, of course. Right? Of course. Security. Yeah, there you go. Security must be involved in any sort of the implementation of any sort of authentication system. I gotta be honest, I have no idea why ISE squared out either of the section. Could not be more obvious.
